description: Cross-language pin of the CTOX RxDB checkpoint wire shape. Consumed by Rust unit tests (src/core/rxdb/src/storage/sqlite/instance.rs, src/core/rxdb/src/plugins/replication_webrtc/index_mod.rs) and the JS smoke (src/apps/business-os/rxdb/tests/checkpoint-contract-smoke.mjs). A one-sided change to the checkpoint payload, the epoch derivation, or the validity-key formats must turn one of these consumers red.

checkpointStatus.latestLwt: number; the highest _meta.lwt in the collection, 0 when empty
checkpointStatus.latestIdHash: sha256-hex of the latest document id; empty string when the collection has no documents
checkpointStatus.epoch: sha256-hex of the newline-joined epoch input
checkpointStatus.lwtRendering: latestLwt is an f64 rendered via Rust Display inside the epoch input: integer values render WITHOUT a decimal point, matching JS String(Number)

handshake.collectionCheckpoint: collection.checkpoint carries the representative collection's checkpointStatus object
handshake.collectionCheckpoints: per-collection checkpointStatus map keyed by collection name; the key is ABSENT for single-collection rooms
handshake.storageGeneration: string when the native peer advertises a storage generation; JSON null when empty (the key is always present, browsers treat null as absent)
handshake.peerSessionSessionId: peerSession.sessionId is the native per-run session id, string, never empty
handshake.nativeTimeMs: unsigned integer, native wall clock in milliseconds since the unix epoch

v2.requires: capability advertised AND non-empty storageGeneration AND non-empty schemaHash; independent of peerSession.sessionId. The current collection checkpoint epoch is appended so a native projection rewrite invalidates retained browser heads without forcing a resync on every daemon restart.

v1.requires: fallback when the v2 path is unavailable; non-empty checkpoint.epoch AND peerSession.sessionId AND schemaHash, else empty key (no checkpoint reuse)
